reasoning: The document has excellent text fidelity, but automated structural checks identified a major defect with incomplete, truncated image alt text on page 1. Additionally, the LLM evaluation noted minor losses of visual emphasis (bolding and underlining) that may affect readability. Because there is one major programmatic defect, the document requires human review to correct the accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 1 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (1 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...r a green star next to an ascending blue staircase"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=1 (truncated=1, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

In the source PDF on page 3, several list items and phrases have strong structural emphasis (bolding and underlining). For instance, 'Application deadline of 4/18/2022 5PM...' is both bolded and underlined in the source. Similarly, 'Subject Line: PFAS Data Application' and the dates in the 'Important Dates' section are heavily bolded. The extraction JSON converts these to plain text without any markdown markers (e.g., **bold**) or HTML tags, resulting in a loss of visual emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Application deadline of 4/18/2022 5PM (or may remain open until we have suitable applicant pool)
concerns.output_quote: "Application deadline of 4/18/2022 5PM (or may remain open until we have suitable applicant pool)"
concerns.location: Page 3, first bulleted list, second item

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 2 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=2, examples=['https://deq.nc.gov/', 'mailto:DEQInternships@ncdenr.gov']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

action_items: Rewrite the truncated alt text for the image on page 1 to provide a complete and meaningful description.
Restore bolding and underlining to critical text elements (e.g., application deadlines) if necessary for emphasis.
Reintegrate standalone hyperlinks into their appropriate inline paragraph context.
